title: The One Where Chandler Gets Caught
weakest_section: 12:10-12:25 range with Ugly Naked Guy gags

jokes.text: This cake is amazing! My God, get a room. I would get a room with this cake. I think I could show this cake a good time.
jokes.setup: Monica expressing enthusiasm about cake, someone sarcastically saying 'get a room'
jokes.punchline: Monica doubling down on the sexual metaphor with cake
jokes.explanation: Good escalating absurdity and character-specific food obsession for Monica

jokes.text: If you had to, what would you give up, food or sex? Sex. Seriously, answer faster.
jokes.setup: Hypothetical choice between food and sex
jokes.punchline: Immediate choice of sex, then complaint about speed of answer
jokes.explanation: Clean setup/punchline with good timing joke

jokes.text: Oh, I'm sorry, honey. But when she said 'sex,' I wasn't thinking about sex with you.
jokes.setup: Apologizing for quick answer about giving up sex
jokes.punchline: Revealing he wasn't thinking about sex with his partner
jokes.explanation: Brutally honest and uncomfortable revelation that's perfectly delivered

jokes.text: It's like a giant hug.
jokes.setup: Continuing food vs sex discussion
jokes.punchline: Describing food as 'a giant hug'
jokes.explanation: Sweet sentiment but not particularly funny

jokes.text: How about sex or dinosaurs?
jokes.setup: Ross choosing food over sex
jokes.punchline: Suggesting an even harder choice for Ross involving dinosaurs
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross character integration - his dinosaur obsession is core to his identity

jokes.text: Oh, my God, it's like Sophie's Choice.
jokes.setup: Being asked to choose between sex and dinosaurs
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to the tragic Holocaust film
jokes.explanation: Hilariously overblown comparison that perfectly captures Ross's dramatic nature

jokes.text: I don't know, it's too hard. No, you gotta pick one. Food. No, sex. Food. Sex. Food. Sex-- I don't know! Oh, God, I want both!
jokes.setup: Joey being asked to choose between food and sex
jokes.punchline: His inability to choose, frantically going back and forth
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey characterization - his love of both food and sex makes this impossible

jokes.text: I want-- I want girls on bread!
jokes.setup: Joey's frustration at having to choose between food and sex
jokes.punchline: His solution: combining both desires literally
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Joey line that's become iconic - perfectly absurd and character-specific

jokes.text: She looks just like a little doll. Oh, no, that is a doll.
jokes.setup: Someone commenting on how cute Emma looks in photos
jokes.punchline: Realizing they're actually looking at a doll
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection but fairly predictable baby/doll mix-up

jokes.text: Oh, thank God, because that thing's really creepy.
jokes.setup: Realizing they were looking at a doll, not Emma
jokes.punchline: Relief that it's not actually the baby that looks creepy
jokes.explanation: Decent follow-up to the doll joke but not particularly memorable

jokes.text: Well, sorry, but it's hard to believe that anyone would tell a story that dull... just to tell it.
jokes.setup: Defending her theory about Ross trying to kill her
jokes.punchline: Suggesting Ross's boring stories must have ulterior motives
jokes.explanation: Clever dig at Ross's tendency toward boring stories, perfectly delivered by Phoebe

jokes.text: Well, it's a shame that you'll miss the movie... because we were gonna see, you know, either Liar Liar... or Betrayal or An Affair to Remember.
jokes.setup: Phoebe testing Chandler who claims to be at work
jokes.punchline: Suggesting movies with titles that relate to cheating/lying
jokes.explanation: Clever way to test Chandler with thematically appropriate movie titles

jokes.text: Those are all really old. Okay, then maybe it'll be-- Dude, Where's My Car? What? They're in a car.
jokes.setup: Chandler pointing out the movies are old, Phoebe trying to think of another
jokes.punchline: Phoebe picking a movie title that matches their current situation
jokes.explanation: Decent situational humor but feels a bit forced

jokes.text: Okay, quick. We gotta find a cab and follow them. Yeah, okay. Let me just grab my night-vision goggles and my stun gun. I got them.
jokes.setup: Phoebe wanting to follow Chandler in a cab
jokes.punchline: Rachel sarcastically mentioning spy gear, Phoebe actually having it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe moment - her having actual spy gear is wonderfully absurd

jokes.text: Isn't it? And what about the amazing wanes coating... the crown molding and the dormer windows in the attic? And the wiggle woms and the zip zorps!
jokes.setup: Monica describing house features enthusiastically
jokes.punchline: Adding completely made-up architectural terms
jokes.explanation: Monica's perfectionist personality making her fake knowledge about house features

jokes.text: This is bringing out a lovely color in you.
jokes.setup: Monica getting worked up about the house
jokes.punchline: Chandler's dry observation about her emotional state
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler dry wit, understated but effective

jokes.text: I don't know what you mean, giant, talking cigarette.
jokes.setup: Chandler asking how badly she wants to smoke
jokes.punchline: Monica's nicotine withdrawal making her see Chandler as a cigarette
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd visualization of nicotine withdrawal, very quotable

jokes.text: If each of you guys would like to pitch in 100 bucks, that would be great.
jokes.setup: Friends questioning their expensive cab adventure to Westchester
jokes.punchline: Rachel casually asking for money to cover the cost
jokes.explanation: Good practical joke about the cost of their spy mission

jokes.text: Why? Who'd you see him with? No one. I'm just saying if we did-- Tell me what you know! No one! Nothing! Mike's a great guy! It was hypothetical! Who did you see him with?
jokes.setup: Rachel asking hypothetical about whether they'd want to know about Mike cheating
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's immediate panic and repeated questioning
jokes.explanation: Good escalating paranoia bit that shows Phoebe's insecurity

jokes.text: If only there were a smaller one to clean this one.
jokes.setup: Monica cleaning, being obsessive about cleanliness
jokes.punchline: Wishing for an even smaller cleaning tool
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica obsessive-compulsive humor about cleaning

jokes.text: Did you think the neighborhood was homey?
jokes.setup: Friends telling Monica that Chandler might be having an affair
jokes.punchline: Monica completely missing the point and asking about the neighborhood
jokes.explanation: Great awkward moment where Monica's excitement about house hunting overrides the affair accusation

jokes.text: Is it me, or have the greetings gone downhill around here?
jokes.setup: Ross calling Chandler 'son of a bitch' when he enters
jokes.punchline: Chandler's understated observation about the hostile greeting
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler deadpan wit in the face of hostility

jokes.text: I knew he couldn't be with a woman for 45 minutes!
jokes.setup: Learning Chandler wasn't having an affair
jokes.punchline: Ross's 'relief' based on Chandler's supposed sexual inadequacy
jokes.explanation: Good character-based insult that reveals Ross's relief and competitive nature

jokes.text: Shouldn't we all vote on stuff like this?
jokes.setup: Monica and Chandler announcing they want to move
jokes.punchline: Joey treating it like a group decision
jokes.explanation: Sweet Joey moment showing how he sees them as a family unit

jokes.text: So you want to buy a house in the '50s.
jokes.setup: Monica describing wanting a lawn, swing set, and ice cream truck
jokes.punchline: Ross pointing out how idealized/outdated their vision is
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about their unrealistic suburban fantasy

jokes.text: What if you want Chinese food at 5 a.m.? Or a fake Rolex that breaks as soon as it rains? Or an Asian hooker sent right to your door?
jokes.setup: Joey arguing against leaving the city
jokes.punchline: His increasingly inappropriate examples of city 'benefits'
jokes.explanation: Good escalating list of increasingly questionable city advantages, very Joey

jokes.text: No! No, it's not! Don't listen to him. I'm gonna thump you.
jokes.setup: Someone saying it's okay for Monica and Chandler to look for houses
jokes.punchline: Joey's childish threat and escalating panic
jokes.explanation: Joey's childish reaction showing his desperation to keep friends close

jokes.text: Bet you wish I was having an affair now, huh?
jokes.setup: Revealing they already made an offer on a house
jokes.punchline: Chandler suggesting an affair would be preferable news
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that reframes the earlier plot - great Chandler wit

jokes.text: Oh, again with the nature. What are you, beavers?
jokes.setup: Monica describing streams and maple trees
jokes.punchline: Ross comparing them to beavers for loving nature
jokes.explanation: Good Ross snark about their suburban dreams

jokes.text: Is this because I come over here without knocking and eat your food? Because I can stop doing that. I really, really think I can.
jokes.setup: Joey desperately trying to understand why they want to move
jokes.punchline: His doubt about his ability to stop his bad habits
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey self-awareness combined with his inability to change

jokes.text: I can't believe Chandler's screwing this woman... but, man, this would be a nice place to live!
jokes.setup: Phoebe remembering her thoughts when they saw the house
jokes.punchline: Her conflicted reaction to the affair vs. the real estate
jokes.explanation: Great Phoebe moment showing her practical priorities even during moral outrage

jokes.text: All my life, everyone's always told me, 'You're a shoe!' 'You're a shoe! You're a shoe. You're a shoe!' I stopped and said, 'What if I don't want to be a shoe? What if I want to be a purse? Or a hat?'
jokes.setup: Rachel having what seems like an emotional conversation
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous metaphor about shoes, purses, and hats
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd metaphor that sounds profound but means nothing

jokes.text: You can see where he'd have trouble.
jokes.setup: Rachel's confusing shoe metaphor
jokes.punchline: Ross's understated acknowledgment of its nonsensical nature
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man reaction to Rachel's absurd metaphor

jokes.text: You know what I just realized? 'Joker' is 'poker' with a 'J'! Coincidence? That's 'joincidence' with a 'C'!
jokes.setup: Chandler making an observation about word similarities
jokes.punchline: Creating the nonsense word 'joincidence'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Chandler wordplay that became iconic - perfect combination of clever and silly

jokes.text: Look! Ugly Naked Guy lit a bunch of candles.
jokes.setup: Ongoing Ugly Naked Guy neighbor references
jokes.punchline: Visual of him with romantic candles
jokes.explanation: Standard running gag callback, not particularly clever

jokes.text: That had to hurt!
jokes.setup: Ugly Naked Guy with candles
jokes.punchline: Implying he burned himself somehow
jokes.explanation: Weak physical comedy follow-up, relies on basic slapstick

jokes.text: No, Mon. You want to put them in concentric circles. I want to do this.
jokes.setup: Monica being controlling about Thanksgiving preparation
jokes.punchline: Rachel calling out Monica's controlling nature
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamic showing Monica's perfectionism and Rachel's resistance

jokes.text: Of course it smells bad. You have your head up a turkey's ass.
jokes.setup: Joey stuck in turkey saying it smells bad
jokes.punchline: Chandler's matter-of-fact explanation
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler deadpan logic about the obvious

jokes.text: Unbelievable! Eleven is correct.
jokes.setup: Monica categorizing towels into multiple categories
jokes.punchline: Ross's amazement at the absurd number of towel categories
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica obsessive-compulsive behavior taken to absurd extreme

jokes.text: That's not even a word!
jokes.setup: Monica guessing Chandler's job as 'transponster'
jokes.punchline: Realizing she made up a word in desperation
jokes.explanation: Great callback to the ongoing joke about nobody knowing what Chandler does

jokes.text: You are mean boys who are just being mean!
jokes.setup: Monica losing the apartment in the trivia contest
jokes.punchline: Her childish complaint about losing
jokes.explanation: Monica's competitive nature leading to childish behavior when losing

jokes.text: Listen, we had a good run. What was it? Four, five months? That's more than most people have in a lifetime. Goodbye.
jokes.setup: Ross discovering Chandler and Monica together
jokes.punchline: Chandler's dramatic goodbye suggesting their relationship was brief
jokes.explanation: Great Chandler panic response, overly dramatic and self-deprecating

jokes.text: Dude, he's right there.
jokes.setup: Chandler confessing to Joey that Ross knows about him and Monica
jokes.punchline: Joey pointing out Ross is standing right there
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective pointing out of the obvious awkwardness

jokes.text: I was gonna go across the hall and write that on Chandler.
jokes.setup: Monica labeling a box 'What were you thinking?'
jokes.punchline: Planning to write the same message on Chandler himself
jokes.explanation: Monica's anger leading to creative revenge planning

jokes.text: She gets tons of catalogs and folds down the pages of things I'd like.
jokes.setup: Monica trying to convince Phoebe that Rachel has good roommate qualities
jokes.punchline: A very specific and mundane 'positive' quality
jokes.explanation: Sweet detail that shows their friendship in a specific, believable way

jokes.text: I think because it didn't involve divorce... or projectile vomiting.
jokes.setup: Chandler saying this has been a great Thanksgiving
jokes.punchline: Comparing it favorably to his usual horrible holidays
jokes.explanation: Good Chandler backstory reference with specific, vivid details

jokes.text: You don't wanna live in Westchester. That's the worst of the Chesters.
jokes.setup: Joey trying to convince them not to move to Westchester
jokes.punchline: Making up a ranking system for places ending in 'chester'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Joey logic creating a nonsensical but convincing hierarchy

jokes.text: Yeah. I'm even more sorry that phone call didn't come... before I told you about looking through the window.
jokes.setup: Joey saying he's sorry about them not getting the house
jokes.punchline: Admitting he wishes the timing had saved him from awkwardness
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware Joey moment about his poor timing

jokes.text: Yeah, and about this Nancy.... If you're not sleeping with her, should I?
jokes.setup: Joey acknowledging Nancy the realtor
jokes.punchline: Immediately asking if he can pursue her
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey opportunism and inability to read the room

jokes.text: Not it! Not it! Damn it!
jokes.setup: Deciding who has to tell their friends about getting the house
jokes.punchline: Playing a childish game to avoid the responsibility
jokes.explanation: Childish behavior from adults facing difficult conversation

tmdb_overview: Phoebe and Rachel follow Chandler with an unknown woman, fearing infidelity, but discover they're house hunting in the suburbs.
